title: ISE Hybrid Deployment — On-Prem PAN to Cloud-PSN Latency and Bandwidth

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.28 third-party ICT risk — cloud-hosting tunnel health.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Drop the VPN tunnel between on-prem and cloud. ISE lag spikes. UC-17.1.62 fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Restore tunnel. Lag returns to normal.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` for replication-lag fields. Network metrics for the on-prem→cloud path: VPN tunnel state from `index=network sourcetype=cisco:asa` or `cisco:ftd`; tunnel BW from `index=cloud sourcetype=aws:cloudwatch`.
app: `Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services` + Cisco firewall TA + cloud TA.
spl:
(index=network sourcetype IN ("cisco:asa","cisco:ftd") "%ASA-3-713")
  OR (index=cloud sourcetype="aws:cloudwatch" metric_name="TunnelState")
| stats sum(eval(like(_raw, "%713%"))) as tunnel_drops, max(value) as tunnel_state by tunnel_id
| where tunnel_drops > 0 OR tunnel_state==0
description: Detects VPN-tunnel issues affecting hybrid on-prem PAN ↔ cloud PSN ISE deployments. When the VPN drops, cloud PSNs lose access to the PAN's policy updates and identity stores.
value: Hybrid ISE deployments depend on stable VPN. A flapping IPsec tunnel manifests as intermittent auth issues at cloud-served sites; this UC catches the network-layer cause directly.
mitreAttack: N/A (data-feed liveness — measures ISE pxGrid topic delivery, no attack mapping)
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Hybrid ISE deployment with VPN tunnel.

Step 1 — Configure firewall + cloud TA.
Step 2 — Search.
Step 3 — Validate.
Step 4 — Dashboard combining tunnel + ISE replication.
Step 5 — Troubleshooting: validate IKE/IPsec config; check NAT-T.
grandmaExplanation: When ISE has parts on the office network and parts in the cloud, they communicate through a secure tunnel. We watch the tunnel — if it breaks, the cloud parts lose their connection home.
